response:
# Perspective Audit — Custom Select Combobox Without Keyboard Navigation

## Step 1 — Escalated perspectives confirmed

Perspectives flagged **MEDIUM or HIGH** by the a11y-critic, reviewed here in full:

- **Keyboard & Motor Access** — HIGH
- **Screen Reader & Semantic Structure** — HIGH

Not reviewed (not escalated): Magnification & Reflow, Environmental Contrast, Vestibular & Motion, Auditory Access, Cognitive & Neurodivergent.

Line citations reference the component code block as read in the audit request (`custom-select-combobox-user.md`).

---

## Step 4 — Per-perspective checklists

### Perspective 5 — Keyboard & Motor Access

WCAG (AA): 1.3.4, 2.1.1, 2.1.2, 2.4.1, 2.4.3, 2.4.7, 2.4.13, 2.5.1, 2.5.2, 2.5.3, 2.5.7, 2.5.8

| # | Checklist item | Result |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | All interactive elements operable by keyboard (2.1.1) | **FINDING** (KM-1) |
| 2 | No keyboard traps (2.1.2) | PASS — Tab / Shift+Tab always escape the widget (see note) |
| 3 | Skip link targets main content (2.4.1) | N/A — single component, not a page |
| 4 | Focus order logical (2.4.3) | **FINDING** (KM-3, focus lost after selection); order itself is DOM-logical |
| 5 | Focus indicator ≥2px, ≥3:1 (2.4.7, 2.4.13) | PASS — input `outline: 3px solid #005fcc` (L117-120); options `:focus-visible` 2px + bg (L147-151) |
| 6 | Multi-point gestures have single-pointer alt (2.5.1) | N/A — no gestures |
| 7 | Activation on up-event (2.5.2) | PASS — `onClick` fires on up-event (L78) |
| 8 | Visible label matches accessible name (2.5.3) | PASS — input labeled by `<label htmlFor>` (L55) |
| 9 | Drag has click alternative (2.5.7) | N/A — no drag |
| 10 | Targets ≥24×24px (2.5.8) | PASS — input ~40px tall; options ~35px tall × 280px (CSS L108-115, L136-140) |
| 11 | Portrait + landscape (1.3.4) | PASS — no orientation lock |

**Red-flag check (auto-CRITICAL):** Interactive element *unreachable* by keyboard — does NOT fire (options are reachable via Tab). Keyboard trap — does NOT fire. Drag-only — N/A. `outline:none` with no replacement — does NOT fire (indicators present). **No keyboard red flag fires.**

*Note on 2.1.2:* No trap exists, but the dropdown never closes on blur (`isOpen` is cleared only in `handleSelect`, L41). After Tab-away the list stays rendered (`position:absolute; z-index:10`) over following content. Not a trap; captured as KM-2.

---

**Finding:** Listbox options cannot be navigated or activated by keyboard. There is no ArrowDown/ArrowUp/Home/End handling; `handleKeyDown` (L47-51) only selects when `filtered.length === 1` and Enter is pressed *while focus is on the input*. Each option is a `<div tabIndex={0} onClick>` (L75-81) with no key handler, so a focused option ignores Enter and Space (Space instead scrolls the page — no `preventDefault`). The only working keyboard path is "type until exactly one option remains, then press Enter on the input" — undiscoverable, and unavailable to a user who wants to browse the presented options rather than type an exact known label.
**WCAG:** 2.1.1 Keyboard (Level A)
**Perspective:** Keyboard & Motor Access
**Severity:** MAJOR
**Route to:** Front-End Dev (secondary: UX Design)
**Evidence:** `handleKeyDown` L47-51 (Enter-only, gated on single match); option `<div tabIndex={0} onClick={...}>` L75-81 with no `onKeyDown`; comment L45-46 confirms arrow/Escape handling absent.
**Fix:** Implement the WAI-ARIA APG *Editable Combobox With List Autocomplete* keyboard model on the input: ArrowDown/ArrowUp move a virtual "active" option via `aria-activedescendant`, Home/End jump to first/last, Enter selects the active option, Escape collapses. Remove `tabIndex={0}` from the options — combobox options must NOT be individual tab stops; focus stays on the input and the active option is tracked with `aria-activedescendant` (see SR-1). This single change fixes both the dead-Enter/Space affordance and the wrong focus model.

> Severity rationale (calibrated, not rule-weighted): rated MAJOR rather than CRITICAL because a keyboard user *can* complete the task — every label is unique, so over-typing to a single match plus Enter always works. It is not "blocked entirely," but the presented affordance (focusable options) is inert and the expected combobox interaction is absent. Ship-blocking (REVISE-level) on its own.

---

**Finding:** The dropdown cannot be dismissed by keyboard. There is no Escape handler (comment L46 confirms), and `isOpen` is set false only on selection (`handleSelect`, L41). A keyboard user who opens the list and decides not to choose has no way to collapse it except by selecting an option or Tabbing away — and Tabbing away leaves the list visually open, floating over subsequent content.
**WCAG:** 2.1.1 Keyboard (Level A) — the "dismiss the popup" function is not keyboard-operable
**Perspective:** Keyboard & Motor Access
**Severity:** MINOR
**Route to:** Front-End Dev (secondary: UX Design)
**Evidence:** No `Escape` branch in `handleKeyDown` (L47-51); `setIsOpen(false)` appears only in `handleSelect` (L41); no `onBlur` collapse on input.
**Fix:** Add `if (e.key === 'Escape') { setIsOpen(false); }` to the input's key handler, and collapse on blur-out of the component. Restore the input's prior value or clear the active-option state on Escape per APG.

---

**Finding:** Focus is lost after selecting an option by pointer. `handleSelect` (L38-43) sets `isOpen` false, which unmounts the dropdown; the just-clicked option `<div>` held focus (activating a `tabIndex=0` element focuses it), so when it unmounts the browser drops focus to `document.body`. The next Tab restarts from the top of the page. (The input-Enter path is unaffected because focus already sits on the input, which is not unmounted.)
**WCAG:** 2.4.3 Focus Order (Level A)
**Perspective:** Keyboard & Motor Access
**Severity:** MINOR
**Route to:** Front-End Dev (secondary: UX Design)
**Evidence:** `handleSelect` L38-43 has no focus-return call; option is `tabIndex={0}` (L79) and is removed when `isOpen` becomes false (L70).
**Fix:** After selection, return focus to the input (`inputRef.current.focus()`) inside `handleSelect`. Once options are no longer tab stops (KM-1 fix), this is fully resolved because focus never leaves the input.

---

### Perspective 6 — Screen Reader & Semantic Structure

WCAG (AA): 1.1.1, 1.3.1, 1.3.2, 2.4.2, 2.4.4, 2.4.6, 3.1.1, 3.1.2, 3.3.1, 3.3.2, 4.1.2, 4.1.3

| # | Checklist item | Result |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | Landmark regions (1.3.1) | N/A (page-level); widget structure handled under 4.1.2 / SR-2 |
| 2 | Heading hierarchy (2.4.6) | N/A — no headings |
| 3 | Informative images have alt (1.1.1) | N/A — no images |
| 4 | DOM order matches visual order (1.3.2) | PASS — input → dropdown → options in source matches visual (L57-85) |
| 5 | Unique descriptive `<title>` (2.4.2) | N/A — page-level |
| 6 | Link text describes destination (2.4.4) | N/A — no links |
| 7 | `lang` on `<html>` (3.1.1) | N/A — page-level, not determinable from component |
| 8 | Foreign phrases wrapped in `lang` (3.1.2) | N/A — labels are English |
| 9 | Input has visible label, programmatically associated (3.3.2) | PASS — `<label htmlFor="country-input">` + `id="country-input"` (L55, L59) |
| 10 | Errors identify field via `aria-describedby` (3.3.1) | N/A — no validation/error state |
| 11 | Custom widgets use correct ARIA roles (4.1.2) | **FINDING** (SR-1, SR-2) |
| 12 | Status messages use `aria-live` (4.1.3) | **FINDING** (SR-3) |
| 13 | SPA route change management | N/A — no routing |

**Red-flag check (auto-CRITICAL):** No `<main>` — N/A (component). Input without any label — does NOT fire (label present, PASS). **Custom widget with no ARIA role — FIRES:** the input (combobox), the dropdown (listbox), and each option all lack roles. Missing `lang` — N/A. **Red flag fires → CRITICAL.**

---

**Finding:** The text input is not exposed as a combobox. It carries no `role="combobox"` (comment L56 confirms), no `aria-expanded` to convey open/closed, no `aria-controls` pointing at the option list, no `aria-activedescendant` to convey which option is active, and no `aria-autocomplete="list"` (comment L67 confirms). To a screen reader the control announces only as "edit text"; the appearance and state of the suggestion list are entirely imperceptible. The user has no way to know suggestions exist, how many there are, or which one is active.
**WCAG:** 4.1.2 Name, Role, Value (Level A)
**Perspective:** Screen Reader & Semantic Structure
**Severity:** CRITICAL (red flag: custom widget with no ARIA role)
**Route to:** Front-End Dev (secondary: Content Author for announced text)
**Evidence:** `<input>` L57-68 — no `role`, `aria-expanded`, `aria-controls`, `aria-activedescendant`, or `aria-autocomplete`; inline comments L56 and L67 confirm the omissions.
**Fix:** Add `role="combobox"`, `aria-expanded={isOpen}`, `aria-controls="<listbox-id>"`, `aria-autocomplete="list"`, and `aria-activedescendant` set to the active option's id (driven by the arrow-key handler from KM-1). Give the input `aria-haspopup="listbox"` for redundancy.

---

**Finding:** The suggestion list and its items have no listbox semantics. The container is a plain `<div className="select-dropdown">` with no `role="listbox"` (comment L71 confirms); each item is a plain `<div className="select-option">` with no `role="option"` and no `aria-selected` (comment L74 confirms). The current selection is signaled only visually — the `.selected` class applies bold + background (CSS L153-156) — with no programmatic equivalent, so the "these are choosable options, and this one is chosen" relationship is not exposed to assistive technology.
**WCAG:** 4.1.2 Name, Role, Value (Level A); 1.3.1 Info and Relationships (Level A) for the visual-only selected state
**Perspective:** Screen Reader & Semantic Structure
**Severity:** CRITICAL (red flag: custom widget with no ARIA role)
**Route to:** Front-End Dev (secondary: Content Author)
**Evidence:** dropdown `<div>` L72 — no `role`; option `<div>` L75-81 — no `role="option"`, no `aria-selected`; selected styling is class-only (L77, CSS L153-156).
**Fix:** Set `role="listbox"` with a stable `id` on the container and a label (`aria-label` or `aria-labelledby` to the field label). Give each item `role="option"`, a unique `id` referenced by `aria-activedescendant`, and `aria-selected={selected?.value === option.value}`. Keep the visual `.selected` class as reinforcement, not the sole signal.

---

**Finding:** No live region announces filtering state. As the user types, `filtered` recomputes (L34-36) and the visible option count changes (8 → n → 1 → 0), but nothing is announced to a screen reader; there is no `aria-live` region (comment L87 confirms). The zero-match case is silent too — when `filtered.length === 0` the dropdown does not render (L70), so a screen reader user receives no "no results" feedback and no signal that further typing is futile. Selection likewise has no explicit announcement.
**WCAG:** 4.1.3 Status Messages (Level AA)
**Perspective:** Screen Reader & Semantic Structure
**Severity:** MAJOR
**Route to:** Front-End Dev (secondary: Content Author for announcement wording)
**Evidence:** No `aria-live`/`role="status"` node anywhere in the returned markup (L53-89); comment L87 confirms the omission; empty-result branch produces no DOM (L70).
**Fix:** Add a visually-hidden `aria-live="polite"` region that announces the result count as the filter changes (e.g., "8 countries available", "1 country available", "No matches") and confirms the chosen value on selection. Debounce so rapid keystrokes do not flood the buffer.

---

## Step 5 — Code-level vs content-level split

All five findings are **code-level** (enforceable from source): missing roles/ARIA properties, missing key handlers, missing live region, and focus handling are all fully determinable from the component and CSS. There are **no content-level findings** — this component has no captions, transcripts, alt text, or editorial prose whose quality would require human review. The only human-verification item is confirming *actual* AT output (below).

---

## Step 5 (contract) — New A11y Evidence Finding blocks (CRITICAL / MAJOR)

### A11y Evidence Finding

wcag_or_apg: WCAG 2.2 4.1.2 Name, Role, Value / WAI-ARIA APG Editable Combobox With List Autocomplete

perspective_alarms: keyboard_motor=HIGH, screen_reader_semantic=HIGH

actual_behavior: control announced as plain edit text; suggestion list and its state imperceptible to AT
trend: new

reproduction_steps: source review only; confirm with a screen reader that options are not reported as a listbox with a selected item

### A11y Evidence Finding

severity: MAJOR
perspective_alarms: screen_reader_semantic=HIGH

reproduction_steps: source review only; confirm with a screen reader that result count / no-results / selection are not announced while typing

### A11y Evidence Finding
finding_id: kbd-no-listbox-navigation
fingerprint: 2f8b0a41d6e37c90
source: perspective-audit Keyboard & Motor Access checklist
wcag_or_apg: WCAG 2.2 2.1.1 Keyboard / WAI-ARIA APG Editable Combobox With List Autocomplete keyboard interaction
section_508_fpc_context: Revised Section 508 incorporates WCAG 2.0 A/AA; 2.1.1 is in scope
severity: MAJOR
perspective_alarms: keyboard_motor=HIGH
evidence: handleKeyDown L47-51 Enter-only and gated on filtered.length===1; option divs L75-81 tabIndex=0 with onClick but no onKeyDown; comments L45-46 confirm arrow/Escape absent
reproduction_steps: source review only; keyboard walkthrough — focus input, type "united" (2 matches), press Enter (nothing), Tab to an option, press Enter/Space (nothing)
expected_behavior: ArrowUp/Down/Home/End move active option; Enter selects active; options are not tab stops
actual_behavior: options tabbable but inert to Enter/Space; only over-typing to a single match + Enter on the input selects
trend: new

---

## Step 6 — Perspective Audit Summary

**Component/artifact reviewed:** Custom Select Combobox (`CustomSelect`) — editable filtering combobox

**Perspectives escalated for deep review:** Keyboard & Motor Access (HIGH); Screen Reader & Semantic Structure (HIGH)

**Perspectives at LOW (not reviewed):** Magnification & Reflow; Environmental Contrast; Vestibular & Motion; Auditory Access; Cognitive & Neurodivergent

### Findings by Severity

| Severity | Count |
|---|---|
| CRITICAL | 2 |
| MAJOR | 2 |
| MINOR | 2 |
| ENHANCEMENT | 0 |

### Perspectives That Passed

None passed clean — both escalated perspectives produced findings. Notable checklist items that **did pass** and should be preserved through remediation:

- Focus indicators on both the input (3px outline) and options (`:focus-visible` 2px + background) meet 2.4.7 (Keyboard).
- Programmatic label association via `htmlFor`/`id` meets 3.3.2 (Screen Reader).
- DOM order matches visual order — 1.3.2 (Screen Reader).
- Target sizes, up-event activation, and no keyboard trap — 2.5.8 / 2.5.2 / 2.1.2 (Keyboard).

### Perspectives With Findings — Grouped by ARRM Role

**Front-End Dev** (primary owner of every finding — all are implementation/semantics/keyboard defects)
- Listbox options not keyboard-navigable or -operable (KM-1) — Keyboard & Motor — MAJOR — 2.1.1 Keyboard
- Dropdown not dismissible by keyboard / no Escape (KM-2) — Keyboard & Motor — MINOR — 2.1.1 Keyboard
- Focus lost to document.body after selection (KM-3) — Keyboard & Motor — MINOR — 2.4.3 Focus Order
- Input not exposed as combobox (SR-1) — Screen Reader & Semantic — CRITICAL — 4.1.2 Name, Role, Value
- Dropdown/options lack listbox & option roles; selection visual-only (SR-2) — Screen Reader & Semantic — CRITICAL — 4.1.2 (+1.3.1)
- No live region for filter count / no-results / selection (SR-3) — Screen Reader & Semantic — MAJOR — 4.1.3 Status Messages

**UX Design** (secondary — owns the intended interaction model)
- Combobox keyboard interaction model (arrow navigation, Escape, focus return) — informs KM-1, KM-2, KM-3.

**Visual Design**
- No findings. Focus indicators and target sizes meet AA; preserve them.

**Content Author** (secondary)
- Wording of the live-region announcements introduced by SR-3 (result-count and no-results phrasing) is a content decision.

### Open Questions Requiring Human Verification

- **Actual AT output** — source review shows the widget is semantically silent (no roles, no live region), which strongly implies screen readers announce only "edit text" and never surface the list. Confirm with NVDA + Firefox, JAWS + Chrome, and VoiceOver + Safari that (a) the suggestion list is imperceptible and (b) selection is not clearly announced. [Human verification required — cannot be confirmed from source]
- **Announcement copy** — the exact phrasing of the SR-3 live-region messages (e.g., singular/plural country wording, no-results text) needs a content author's sign-off. [Human verification required — cannot be confirmed from source]

### Recommended Next Step

**BLOCK** — Two CRITICAL screen-reader findings (SR-1, SR-2; the "custom widget with no ARIA role" red flag fires) make the combobox unusable with assistive technology. Do not ship until the combobox/listbox/option roles, states, and keyboard interaction model are implemented per the WAI-ARIA APG *Editable Combobox With List Autocomplete* pattern, and a live region (SR-3) is added. The MAJOR keyboard finding (KM-1) is resolved by the same APG implementation; address the two MINOR keyboard findings (KM-2, KM-3) in the same pass. Re-test with a11y-test (axe-core + real keyboard walkthrough) and a manual AT pass after the fix.

_benchmark.mechanism: Claude Code Agent(general-purpose, model=opus), background, one per fixture
_benchmark.protocol_note: prompts composed identically to run_cloud_benchmark.py build_escalation_prompt EXCEPT fixture truncated before '## Accessibility Issues' (answer key withheld — prior lanes were non-blind)
